msghub.i18n.IngestStates.ui.presets.name: Name
msghub.i18n.IngestStates.ui.templateCata: Name or alias of the ioBroker state this rule monitors. Renders as the configured display name. Example: `Living room · Temperature`.
Monitored state
Date and time when the monitored state returned to normal. Formatted as a readable date/time string. Only present in recovery messages.
Recovered at
Raw value of the monitored state at the moment the rule was triggered. Example: `23.5`.
Current value
Date and time when the current cycle window began (i.e. when the counter was last reset). Formatted as a readable date/time string.
Cycle start
Number of state changes required to complete one cycle. The rule fires when the actual count falls short of this value. Example: `25` means 25 changes are expected per window.
Expected changes
How many more state changes are still needed to satisfy the current cycle. Example: `7` means 7 more changes are required before the cycle is considered complete.
Missing changes
Number of state changes recorded within the current cycle window. Example: `18` means 18 changes have been counted since the cycle started.
Changes so far
Date and time by which the required cycle activity must be completed. After this point the cycle window resets. Formatted as a readable date/time string.
Cycle deadline
Length of the time-based cycle window in milliseconds. Example: `3600000` = 1 hour. Use a format filter like `|duration` to display this as a readable duration.
Cycle duration (ms)
Timestamp of the last actual value change of the ioBroker state (field `lc`) — only updated when the value itself changes, not on every write. Formatted as a readable date/time string.
Last changed
Timestamp of the most recent update of the ioBroker state (field `ts`) — even if the value did not change. Formatted as a readable date/time string.
Last updated
Direction of the detected trend: `up` (rising), `down` (falling), or empty string if the direction could not be determined.
Trend direction
Highest value recorded during the unsettled period. Example: `28.6`.
Maximum during unrest
Lowest value recorded during the unsettled period. Example: `14.1`.
Minimum during unrest
Difference between the highest and lowest values during the unsettled period (max − min). Example: `14.5`.
Total range
Date and time when the unsettled activity or trend was first detected. Formatted as a readable date/time string.
Unrest started at
Value of the monitored state at the moment the unsettled activity or trend began. Example: `18.2`.
Value at start
Calculated cost of energy consumed during the session (energy used × price per kWh). Example: `0.54`.
Session cost
Energy consumed during the session (counter at end minus counter at start). Example: `1.8` (kWh).
Energy used
Value of the energy counter at the moment the session began. Used as the baseline for calculating energy consumed. Example: `12450.3` (kWh).
Counter at session start
The maximum value the state must rise to or above to satisfy the condition (threshold plus hysteresis, if configured). Applies to `gt`, `inside`, and `outside` modes.
Upper limit
The minimum value the state must fall to or below to satisfy the condition (threshold minus hysteresis, if configured). Applies to `lt`, `inside`, and `outside` modes.
Lower limit
Name or alias of the ioBroker state that acts as the trigger for this rule. Example: `Garden gate · Motion`.
Trigger state
The value of the trigger state that activated the rule. Rendered as a string. Example: `true` or `1`.
Trigger value
